close_reason: Arc<dyn SigningBackend> is the established workspace pattern: from_signing_uri() returns Arc<dyn SigningBackend>. Accepting the same type in new() is consistent, not leaked. Changing only this crate would create inconsistency.
description:
All three adapters expose Arc<dyn SigningBackend> in their public constructors and into_inner() methods:
- Ed25519Signer::new(backend: Arc<dyn SigningBackend>) / into_inner() -> Arc<dyn SigningBackend> (lines 122, 133)
- EcdsaP256Signer::new / into_inner (lines 181, 192)
- RsaPssSigner::new / into_inner (lines 247, 258)

Choosing Arc as the sharing mechanism is an implementation detail. If the crate later needs to accept Box<dyn SigningBackend>, a generic B: SigningBackend + Send + Sync, or any other form, every existing call site breaks. The into_inner() return type is also locked — callers who store the returned Arc have a type dependency on Arc specifically.

A less-locking alternative: accept impl Into<Arc<dyn SigningBackend>> or a generic type parameter constrained to AsRef<dyn SigningBackend>, leaving room to widen acceptance later without breaking existing Arc callers.
title: Arc<dyn SigningBackend> in public constructors leaks internal representation
